name: discord-bot-architect
description: Specialized skill for building production-ready Discord bots. Covers Discord.js (JavaScript) and Pycord (Python), gateway intents, slash commands, interactive components, rate limiting, and sharding.
capabilities: Create Discord bots using Discord.js v14 with slash commands and event handling
Build Discord bots using Pycord with application commands and cogs
Implement interactive components including buttons, select menus, and modals
Configure gateway intents properly to avoid privileged intent issues
Set up command and event loading systems with proper file organization
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 45 static findings are false positives. The skill contains standard Discord bot patterns (dynamic require for command loading, fs.readdirSync for directory scanning, environment variable access for bot tokens, and Discord gateway connections). These are required for legitimate Discord bot functionality and do not pose security risks.
valueStatement: Building Discord bots from scratch is complex with many pitfalls around intents, command registration, and rate limiting. This skill provides ready-to-use patterns for Discord.js and Pycord that follow best practices for production deployment.
limitations: Does not cover music bots or voice channel features
Does not include database integration patterns
Does not provide hosting or deployment instructions
Does not cover Discord moderation specific features

useCases.targetUser: Developer new to Discord bot development
useCases.title: Create a new Discord bot project
useCases.description: Quickly scaffold a new Discord bot with proper structure for commands and events

useCases.title: Add interactive UI to existing bot
useCases.description: Enhance a bot with buttons, select menus, and modals for rich user interactions

useCases.title: Migrate from legacy commands to slash commands
useCases.description: Refactor existing bot to use modern slash commands with proper options

promptTemplates.title: Create basic ping command
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the discord-bot-architect skill to create a basic ping command with Discord.js that replies with the bot latency.
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with Discord bot development

promptTemplates.title: Build interactive menu
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the discord-bot-architect skill to create a slash command with button and select menu components that collect user input.

promptTemplates.title: Set up Python bot with cogs
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the discord-bot-architect skill to create a Pycord bot structure using cogs for command organization.

promptTemplates.title: Configure proper intents
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the discord-bot-architect skill to show me how to properly configure gateway intents for a bot that needs message content but wants to minimize privileged intents.

bestPractices: Always use slash commands instead of message content for bot commands
Configure only the minimum required gateway intents to reduce privilege requirements
Separate commands and events into different directories with dynamic loading
Never sync commands on every bot start to avoid rate limits
antiPatterns: Using Message Content Intent for command parsing - deprecated and requires privileged intent
Syncing slash commands on every bot startup - causes rate limiting issues
Blocking the event loop with synchronous operations - causes gateway disconnections
Hardcoding bot tokens in source code - should use environment variables

faq.question: What is the difference between Discord.js and Pycord?
faq.answer: Discord.js is a JavaScript library while Pycord is a Python library. Both provide similar functionality for building Discord bots. Choose based on your preferred programming language.

faq.question: Do I need special permissions to use slash commands?
faq.answer: No, slash commands do not require any special permissions. They are available to all bots and use the Discord application commands API.

faq.question: What are gateway intents?
faq.answer: Gateway intents determine which events your bot receives from Discord. Some intents like Message Content are privileged and require approval in the Developer Portal.

faq.question: How do I deploy slash commands?
faq.answer: Use a separate deploy script to register commands rather than syncing on every startup. Global commands take up to 1 hour to propagate across all servers.

faq.question: Can I use buttons and select menus together?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add up to 5 components per action row. Buttons and select menus can be combined but only one select menu per row is allowed.

faq.question: What are cogs in Pycord?
faq.answer: Cogs are a way to organize commands into separate files or modules. They allow you to group related commands together and load them dynamically.
